title: Reframe as story-aware focus sessions tied to scene objectives
description:
## Problem
Generic writing timers are now category-standard and do not reliably improve story completion quality on their own. Writers need focused sessions that connect directly to scene-level narrative outcomes, not just elapsed time and word counts.

## Target user
Fiction writers who draft in scenes and want structured sessions that help them finish specific narrative objectives (new beat, revision pass, dialogue polish) with less context-switching.

## Outcome / KPI
- Increase % of sessions with completed scene objective
- Increase multi-session continuity rate (users resume the suggested next scene within 24h)
- Improve week-2 retention among users who run story-aware focus sessions

## Scope
- Add `Story-aware Focus Session` mode in the editor with optional timer presets (15/25/45 minutes)
- Require selecting a target scene and objective type before starting (e.g., draft beat, revise pacing, tighten dialogue)
- Show live progress with both words-added and objective status (in progress / complete)
- End session with recap: words added, objective completion, and suggested next-scene handoff note
- Persist recent session history per story, including objective metadata

## Non-goals
- Social leaderboards, streak competitions, or multiplayer battles
- Advanced coaching/critique generated by AI
- Cross-story analytics dashboards in v1

## Acceptance criteria
- User can start/pause/end a focus session tied to a selected scene without data loss
- Objective selection is required at session start and stored in session history
- Session recap includes objective completion status and a next-step handoff note
- Save-state visibility remains present during active session mode
- Session settings/history persist across reload for the same user and story

## Priority rationale
Competitors already offer timer-only sprint tools; reframing around scene-objective completion creates a clearer story-first advantage and aligns with our goal to ship a writer workflow optimized for drafting outcomes.

## Handoff
Assign to: Market Research


<plan>

## Goal
Implement Story-aware Focus Sessions: a session mode in the editor tied to specific scene objectives. Writers select a target scene and objective before starting, see a live overlay with timer and words added, and end with a recap including objective completion status and a next-scene handoff note. Session history persists per story in localStorage.

## Scope and files

**New files:**
- `src/types/session.ts` — FocusSession, ObjectiveType, ActiveSessionState types
- `src/lib/sessions.ts` — localStorage CRUD for session persistence + countWords helper
- `src/hooks/useFocusSession.ts` — session state machine: setup, active timer, recap, history
- `src/components/FocusSession/FocusSessionSetupModal.tsx` + `.module.css`
- `src/components/FocusSession/FocusSessionOverlay.tsx` + `.module.css`
- `src/components/FocusSession/FocusSessionRecapModal.tsx` + `.module.css`
- `src/components/FocusSession/SessionHistoryPanel.tsx` + `.module.css`

**Modified files:**
- `src/app/story/[id]/page.tsx` — wire up hook + components, add Sessions sidebar tab, add Focus Session button
- `src/app/story/[id]/page.module.css` — overlay positioning, focus session button styles

## Step-by-step plan

### Step 1 — Types (src/types/session.ts)

Define the following types:

- `ObjectiveType`: union of 'draft-beat' | 'revise-pacing' | 'tighten-dialogue' | 'polish-description' | 'review-continuity' | 'other'
- `ObjectiveStatus`: 'in-progress' | 'complete'
- `FocusSession` interface: id, storyId, sceneId, sceneName (denormalized), objectiveType, objectiveText (label for named types; freeform for 'other'), startedAt (ms), endedAt (null = active), timerPresetMinutes (0 = no timer), wordsAtStart, wordsAtEnd (null = active), objectiveStatus, handoffNote, nextSceneId
- `ActiveSessionState` interface: sessionId, elapsedSeconds, isPaused, wordsAtStart

### Step 2 — Session persistence (src/lib/sessions.ts)

localStorage key helpers:
- sessionsKey(storyId) => 'story-${storyId}-sessions'
- activeSessionKey(storyId) => 'story-${storyId}-active-session'

Functions:
- `getSessions(storyId): FocusSession[]` — parse JSON, sort by startedAt desc, return max 20
- `saveSession(session: FocusSession): void` — upsert to array by id, trim to 20, persist
- `getActiveSessionState(storyId): ActiveSessionState | null`
- `saveActiveSessionState(storyId, state): void`
- `clearActiveSessionState(storyId): void`
- `countWords(text: string): number` — text.trim().split(/s+/).filter(Boolean).length

### Step 3 — useFocusSession hook (src/hooks/useFocusSession.ts)

Props: `{ storyId, currentContent, currentSceneId, scenes }`

Internal state: setupOpen, activeSession, recapSession, elapsedSeconds, isPaused

On mount: load activeSessionState from localStorage; if present, restore active session from sessions list and set elapsed/paused (reload resilience).

Timer useEffect: when activeSession != null and not isPaused, run setInterval(1000) incrementing elapsedSeconds and persisting saveActiveSessionState every tick. Cleanup clears interval.

Exposed actions:
- openSetup() / closeSetup()
- startSession(config: { sceneId, sceneName, objectiveType, objectiveText, timerPresetMinutes, wordsAtStart }) — creates FocusSession with crypto.randomUUID(), saves to localStorage, sets activeSession, persists active state
- pauseSession() / resumeSession()
- toggleObjectiveStatus() — flips activeSession.objectiveStatus and persists
- endSession() — sets endedAt, wordsAtEnd = countWords(currentContent), moves to recapSession, clears active state
- saveRecap(handoffNote, nextSceneId, objectiveStatus) — finalizes session, calls saveSession(), clears recapSession

Computed: wordsAdded = countWords(currentContent) - (activeSession?.wordsAtStart ?? 0)

### Step 4 — FocusSessionSetupModal

Props: `{ isOpen, scenes, currentSceneId, onStart, onCancel }`

Render using same overlay/dialog pattern as ConfirmDialog (styles.overlay + styles.dialog). Fields:
1. Target scene — select listing all scenes by title; default to currentSceneId if set
2. Objective type — select with 6 options. When "Other" selected, show freeform input maxLength=60 below
3. Timer preset — row of radio buttons: Off | 15 min | 25 min | 45 min | Custom (reveals number input min=1 max=180)
4. Start session button — disabled until scene and objective are selected
5. Cancel button

Behaviour: Escape key calls onCancel. Focus on scene select on open.

onStart payload: `{ sceneId, sceneName, objectiveType, objectiveText, timerPresetMinutes, wordsAtStart: countWords(currentContent) }`

### Step 5 — FocusSessionOverlay

Props: `{ session, elapsedSeconds, isPaused, wordsAdded, onPause, onResume, onEnd, onToggleObjective, scenes, currentSceneId }`

Position: docked to bottom of .editorCanvas via absolute CSS. Non-blocking — does not cover textarea. Use --z-sticky z-index token.

Layout (compact horizontal bar):
- Scene name + objective text
- Timer: if timerPresetMinutes > 0, show countdown (timerPresetMinutes*60 - elapsedSeconds) as M:SS; when reaches 0 auto-call onEnd. Else show elapsed as M:SS.
- Words added: "+N words" (clamp display to >= 0)
- Objective status toggle button: "In Progress" or "Complete"
- Pause/Resume button
- End Session button
- If currentSceneId differs from session.sceneId: subtle muted-text note "Active session targets [session.sceneName]"

Screen reader: include an aria-live="polite" region. Set its text to "5 minutes remaining" once when remaining first crosses below 300 seconds (use a ref to gate the announcement to fire only once).

### Step 6 — FocusSessionRecapModal

Props: `{ session, scenes, onSave, onSaveAndOpenNext, onDismiss }`

Render as modal (same overlay pattern). Fields:
- Read-only summary: scene name, objective type label + "Complete / Incomplete" badge
- Words added: session.wordsAtEnd - session.wordsAtStart
- Duration: Math.round((session.endedAt - session.startedAt) / 60000) minutes
- Next scene select: scenes sorted by order; default to scene with order = current scene order + 1; allow changing via select
- Handoff note textarea: max 200 chars; pre-populate with "Continue [objectiveText] in [nextSceneName]"
- Buttons: "Save and close" | "Save and open next scene"

### Step 7 — SessionHistoryPanel

Props: `{ storyId, scenes }`

On mount and on storyId change: load getSessions(storyId). Render list of session cards (most recent first, max 20):
- Date (use existing relativeTime.ts lib), scene name, objective label, Complete/Incomplete badge, words added, handoff note snippet (truncated at 80 chars)

Empty state: "No sessions recorded yet for this story."

### Step 8 — Wire up in page.tsx

1. Import and call useFocusSession({ storyId: id, currentContent: content, currentSceneId: focusedSceneId, scenes }).
2. In editorHeader: add a "Focus Session" button (before SaveStatus) that calls openSetup(). Style with styles.focusSessionButton.
3. In sidebar tab bar: add 4th tab "Sessions" alongside Notes / Revision / Collab.
4. Render SessionHistoryPanel in the Sessions tabpanel.
5. Render FocusSessionSetupModal (isOpen gates render internally).
6. Render FocusSessionOverlay inside .editorCanvas when activeSession is set (editor mode only).
7. Render FocusSessionRecapModal when recapSession is set. On onSaveAndOpenNext: call saveRecap then router.push to next scene.
8. In page.module.css: add .focusSessionButton token-based styles, set .editorCanvas position: relative for overlay anchoring.

## Test plan

Manual checks:

1. Setup modal opens on button click; Start disabled until scene + objective selected; Custom timer requires positive number; Escape closes; focus traps inside.
2. Active session: overlay appears; countdown timer runs (preset); elapsed timer runs (no preset); words added increments as you type.
3. Pause/Resume: timer pauses; resumes; localStorage activeSessionState.elapsedSeconds reflects pause.
4. Page reload during active session: overlay restores with correct elapsed time; timer resumes.
5. Off-scene note: open a different scene while session active; overlay shows "Active session targets [X]".
6. Timer zero: countdown reaches 0; auto-triggers end session; recap modal appears.
7. Recap modal: shows correct words added, duration, next-scene suggestion, editable handoff note; "Save and close" persists session; "Save and open next scene" navigates.
8. Session history: Sessions tab shows saved sessions most-recent-first; empty state when none; max 20 shown.
9. Save status: SaveStatus component still visible in editor header while session is active.
10. Keyboard: Tab through all modal/overlay controls; Escape on setup modal cancels; Space/Enter on buttons.
11. Screen reader: 5-min warning announced once via aria-live region.

Build check: npm run build with no TypeScript errors.

## Risks and mitigations

| Risk | Mitigation |
|---|---|
| Word count is story-level (one textarea), not per-scene | v1: count words in full story content at start/end. Add TODO comment for per-scene tracking. Per design spec "words added" is not scene-specific. |
| Timer gap on page reload | Restore elapsedSeconds from localStorage; gap equals reload time. Acceptable for v1. |
| setInterval drift | Not critical; timer is indicative not exact for v1. |

## Rollback strategy

All changes are additive. To roll back: revert page.tsx diff, delete new component/lib/hook/type files. No schema or database changes.

## Observability

Follow existing console.log pattern (see scenes.ts):
- On session start: console.log with session id and sceneId
- On session end: console.log with session id and words added delta

## Acceptance mapping

| Acceptance criterion | Implementation |
|---|---|
| User can start/pause/end a focus session tied to a selected scene without data loss | Setup modal validates scene selection; active state persisted to localStorage every second; end session saves to history |
| Objective selection is required at session start and stored in session history | Start button disabled until objective selected; objectiveType + objectiveText stored on FocusSession |
| Session recap includes objective completion status and a next-step handoff note | RecapModal shows objectiveStatus badge, editable handoff note textarea pre-populated with suggestion |
| Save-state visibility remains present during active session mode | SaveStatus stays in editorHeader; overlay is docked to canvas bottom, not replacing header |
| Session settings/history persist across reload for the same user and story | sessions.ts localStorage persistence; active session state restored on hook mount |

## Handoff

Assign to: Code Monkey

</plan>
